title: Slow Roasted Slovakian Pork Roast
description: A comforting Slovakian dish featuring slow roasted pork and green salsa, perfect for a cold evening. This recipe is shojin-friendly and uses fermented ingredients for added depth of flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork roast with salt, black pepper, and caraway seeds.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large oven-safe pot over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork roast until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened.
Add the fermented sauerkraut and green salsa to the pot.
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Roast the pork for 6 hours, or until tender and flaky.
Let the pork rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
